description: Need to add a `last_seen` column to the `devices` table in /home/user/fleet/telemetry.db — should be a timestamp, nullable. Table already has like 200 rows of live device data so don't want to drop anything obviously.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/fleet/telemetry.db is a SQLite3 database file, writable by the agent.
- Contains a single table `devices` with schema: CREATE TABLE devices (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, device_id TEXT NOT NULL, firmware_version TEXT, last_ping INTEGER);
- Table contains exactly 200 rows of device data.
- sqlite3 CLI tool is available.

Expected final state:
- `sqlite3 /home/user/fleet/telemetry.db ".schema devices"` shows a `last_seen` column of some timestamp-compatible type (INTEGER, TEXT, DATETIME, or similar SQLite type affinity), with no NOT NULL constraint.
- `sqlite3 /home/user/fleet/telemetry.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM devices;"` returns 200.
- All original columns (id, device_id, firmware_version, last_ping) still exist with their data intact.

Invariants:
- All 200 original rows preserved with their original values in id, device_id, firmware_version, last_ping columns.
- No other tables created or dropped.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- `sqlite3 /home/user/fleet/telemetry.db "SELECT id, device_id FROM devices ORDER BY id LIMIT 5;"` must return the same values as before the modification (seeded data will have deterministic first 5 rows to verify).
name: task_000000_d32d1268
